abstract.para: Before you can use a resource in the cluster, it must be set up. For example, to use an Apache server as a cluster resource, set up the Apache server first and complete the Apache configuration before starting the respective resource in your cluster.
If a resource has specific environment requirements, make sure they are present and identical on all cluster nodes. This kind of configuration is not managed by &sleha;. You must do this yourself.
You can create primitive resources using either &hawk; or &crmsh;.

important.title: Do not touch services managed by the cluster
important.para: When managing a resource with &sleha;, the same resource must not be started or stopped otherwise (outside of the cluster, for example manually or on boot or reboot). The &ha; software is responsible for all service start or stop actions.

If you need to execute testing or maintenance tasks after the services are already running under cluster control, make sure to put the resources, nodes, or the whole cluster into maintenance mode before you touch any of them manually.

listitem.para: You can perform this procedure on any node in the cluster.

step.para: Log in either as the &rootuser; user or as a user with sudo privileges.
View the required and optional parameters for a resource agent:
Configure a primitive resource. The basic command requires at least a name, a resource agent, and any parameters required by the resource agent:
step.screen: &prompt.user;sudo crm configure primitive RESOURCE_NAME RESOURCE_AGENT \ params ATTRIBUTE=VALUE

listitem.para: Monitor operations can have the following properties:
The action to perform. Common values: monitor, start, stop.
How frequently to perform the operation, in seconds.
How long to wait before declaring the action has failed.

What conditions need to be satisfied before this action occurs. Allowed values: nothing, quorum, fencing. The default depends on whether fencing is enabled and if the resource's class is stonith. For &stonith; resources, the default is nothing.
The action to take if this action ever fails. Allowed values:
ignore: Pretend the resource did not fail.
block: Do not perform any further operations on the resource.
stop: Stop the resource and do not start it elsewhere.
restart: Stop the resource and start it again (possibly on a different node).
fence: Bring down the node on which the resource failed (&stonith;).
standby: Move all resources away from the node on which the resource failed.
Run the operation only if the resource has this role.

step.para: Check the status of the resource:
If the resource is Stopped, you can start it with the following command:

listitem.para: You can access the &hawk; Web interface from any machine that has network access to the nodes.

step.para: In a Web browser, go to https://HAWKSERVER:7630/ and log in.
From the left navigation bar, select Configuration Add Resource.

note.para: &hawk; can't create a cluster resource with the same name as a cluster node.

step.para: Select the resource agent Class: ocf, stonith or systemd.
If you selected the ocf class, select the resource agent Provider.
From the Type list, select a resource agent.
&hawk; displays a description of the selected resource agent.
After you select a resource agent, &hawk; shows the following categories. Either keep these categories as suggested by &hawk;, or edit them as required.

listitem.para: Determines which instance of a service the resource controls. When creating a resource, &hawk; automatically shows any required parameters. Edit them to get a valid resource configuration.
Needed for resource monitoring. When creating a resource, &hawk; displays the most important resource operations (monitor, start and stop).

Tells the CRM how to treat a specific resource. When creating a resource, &hawk; automatically lists the important meta attributes for that resource (for example, the target-role attribute that defines the initial state of a resource. By default, it is set to Stopped, so the resource does not start immediately).
Tells the CRM what capacity a certain resource requires from a node.

step.para: Click Create to finish the configuration.
A message at the top of the screen shows if the action was successful.
